title: Both ends
prompt: Write first_and_last(lst) that returns a 2-item list containing the first and last elements: first_and_last([1,2,3]) is [1, 3]. Assume the list has at least one item.

help.plain: Return a new list with just the first element and the last element.
help.analogy: Bookends — you grab the two ends of the shelf and ignore everything between.
help.mnemonic: Index 0 = first; index -1 = last (negatives count from the back).
help.nudge: How do you get the LAST item without knowing how long the list is?
